title: Sour Fluffy Tunisian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A high-protein-friendly solo-dinner dish featuring sous-vide cooked chicken, chili flakes, and a tangy sauce for a fluffy texture and sour taste.

ingredients.name: sous-vide cooked chicken breast
ingredients.notes: Ensure the chicken is cooked to an internal temperature of at least 74°C (165°F) for food safety.

ingredients.name: fresh garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Mince finely for better flavor integration.

ingredients.name: lime juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed for the best taste and freshness.

ingredients.name: sour cream or yogurt
ingredients.notes: Use plain low-fat sour cream or unsweetened plain yogurt as a healthier option.

ingredients.name: tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Choose tomato paste made from fresh tomatoes for extra flavor.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium soy sauce or tamari as a gluten-free alternative.

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the chili flakes and minced garlic. Set aside.
Place the sous-vide cooked chicken breast in a shallow pan with enough hot water to cover it entirely. Cook for about 15 minutes at an internal temperature of 74°C (165°F).
In a large skillet, heat some vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the prepared chili-garlic mixture and stir until fragrant.
Pour in the tomato paste and soy sauce into the skillet. Stir well to combine with the chicken juices from the sous-vide pan.
Add the fresh lime juice to the skillet. Season with salt and pepper to taste, then let the sauce simmer for a minute or two to thicken slightly.
Gently fold in the sour cream or yogurt until combined. The sauce should be creamy but not too runny.
Serve hot over a bed of steamed rice if desired.
